message:
feat(frankenpandas): expose fp-types helpers (fd90.262 / br-xd0en)

Five fp-types items missing from frankenpandas:

Top-level (5):
- SparseDType (Sparse extension dtype variant)
- cast_scalar_owned (move-semantic cast — README line 769 references
  "AG-03 identity-cast skip via cast_scalar_owned")
- count_na, fill_na, dropna (Vec<Scalar> module-level helpers
  matching pandas' top-level pd.isna/notna shape)

Prelude (3 most user-facing):
- count_na, fill_na, dropna alongside the existing isna/isnull/
  notna/notnull (added by fd90.208)

SparseDType + cast_scalar_owned kept out of prelude — less common.

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-x924","title":"fp-index Index.to_frame (convert to DataFrame) parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:44:37.100223847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:07.249719137Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:07.247938366Z","close_reason":"Scoped out — Index.to_frame belongs in fp-frame (Index→DataFrame crosses crate boundary)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-xaom","title":"interval_range builder (j8k4 Phase 2)","description":"Phase 2 follow-up to br-frankenpandas-j8k4 (Interval scaffolding landed in commit 6e8ec43).

**Scope.** Add `fp_types::interval_range(start, end, periods, closed) -> Vec<Interval>` matching `pd.interval_range(start, end, periods, freq, closed)` for the equal-width numeric case.

**Signatures (phase 2a — equal-width numeric only):**
```rust
pub fn interval_range_by_periods(
    start: f64,
    end: f64,
    periods: usize,
    closed: IntervalClosed,
) -> Vec<Interval>;

pub fn interval_range_by_step(
    start: f64,
    end: f64,
    step: f64,
    closed: IntervalClosed,
) -> Result<Vec<Interval>, TypeError>;
```

**Reference behavior (pandas 2.2):**
- `pd.interval_range(0, 10, periods=5)` → `IntervalIndex([(0.0, 2.0], (2.0, 4.0], (4.0, 6.0], (6.0, 8.0], (8.0, 10.0]])` — N periods = N intervals, equal width.
- `pd.interval_range(0, 10, freq=2)` → same 5 intervals.
- Default `closed='right'` matches pandas.
- Out-of-order (start > end) returns empty.
- Zero periods returns empty.

**Fix shape:**
1. Two new module-level fns in fp-types (near the existing `Interval` impl).
2. Helper that builds N evenly-spaced intervals between start..=end.
3. 6-8 unit tests covering pandas parity: N=1, N=0, reversed range, closed variants, equal-step with trailing-partial-interval rejection.

**Blast radius.** Purely additive in fp-types. Zero existing call-sites touched.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — precondition for the Phase 3 `cut`/`qcut` output-shape change. Landing interval_range first gives other crates a stable builder to consume.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:48:56.468109940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:51:21.140410130Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T00:51:21.137939713Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dtype","interval","parity-gap","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-xarz","title":"Add Series.expanding().corr/cov(other) parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:22:24.482895817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:24:10.086211291Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:24:10.084332815Z","close_reason":"Added Expanding::corr/cov(other) with 4 unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-xd0en","title":"fd90.262: Expose fp-types helpers (count_na/fill_na/dropna/cast_scalar_owned/SparseDType)","description":"fd90.262: Expose fp-types module-level helpers (count_na/fill_na/dropna/cast_scalar_owned) + SparseDType.

Several fp-types public items are missing from frankenpandas:
- count_na, fill_na, dropna (top-level Vec<Scalar> helpers — match pandas' top-level pd.isna/notna pattern)
- cast_scalar_owned (move-semantic cast variant — README line 769 "AG-03 identity-cast skip via cast_scalar_owned")
- SparseDType (Sparse dtype variant payload — README mentions "Sparse" extension dtype)

Scope:
- Add 5 items to top-level fp_types re-export
- Add the 3 Vec<Scalar> helpers (count_na, fill_na, dropna) to prelude
- Skip cast_scalar_owned + SparseDType from prelude (less common)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 10-15min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T07:53:19.505551401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:55:42.757858513Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T07:55:42.755058359Z","close_reason":"Exposed 5 fp-types items at top-level: SparseDType (Sparse extension dtype), cast_scalar_owned (move-semantic cast — README line 769 mentions 'AG-03 identity-cast skip via cast_scalar_owned'), count_na, fill_na, dropna (Vec<Scalar> module-level helpers matching pandas' top-level pd.isna/notna pattern). Added count_na/fill_na/dropna to prelude alongside the existing isna/isnull/notna/notnull. Skipped SparseDType + cast_scalar_owned from prelude (less common). Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard. All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-xd9","title":"Add metamorphic properties for shift composition semantics","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T00:55:14.080445816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T01:00:56.019023734Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T01:00:56.016983529Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-xdolc","title":"fd90.118: README df.crosstab(str, str) wrong — associated fn taking &Series args","description":"fd90.74-117 README audit. Lines 514-515 in DataFrame Output Formats section show:

  let ct = df.crosstab("gender", "department")?;
  let ct_norm = df.crosstab_normalize("gender", "department", "all")?;

Both fail to compile against fp-frame:

- crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:26257 declares
    pub fn crosstab(index_series: &Series, columns_series: &Series)
        -> Result<Self, FrameError>
  This is an ASSOCIATED FUNCTION (no &self), and it takes &Series args, not &str column names.

- crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:26337 declares
    pub fn crosstab_normalize(index_series: &Series, columns_series: &Series, normalize: &str)
        -> Result<Self, FrameError>
  Same — associated fn, &Series args, not &str column names.

A user copy-pasting df.crosstab("gender", "department") would hit:
  error[E0599]: no method named 'crosstab' found for struct 'DataFrame'

Fix: rewrite as DataFrame::crosstab(&series1, &series2)? with explicit Series construction (or extraction from df via df.column / Series::new) so users see the actual contract. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:10:21.954881490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:11:54.374043272Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:11:54.371289917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-xfrv","title":"pandas/io/sql: read_sql(parse_dates=[...]) datetime coercion parity","description":"py signature:
pandas.read_sql("SELECT ts, value FROM t", con, parse_dates=["ts"]) -> DataFrame

Current fp-io SQL reads leave text/integer timestamp fields as raw scalars. Add parse_dates support after result materialization so declared columns are coerced with pandas-like datetime parsing semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:06:31.542378418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:26:09.226161975Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:26:09.224402254Z","close_reason":"Implemented SQL parse_dates parity in commit 4bd78da","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
